title: stachys zepcensis
intro: Stachys zepcensis, also known as Alpine Woundwort, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides and phenolic acids. Consuming Alpine Woundwort may provide health benefits such as wound healing, relief from digestive issues, and the reduction of fever. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Stachys zepcensis are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or externally to promote healing and alleviate various health complaints.
